title: The Wildest Sun A Novel
author: Asha Lemmie
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Wildest Sun A Novel" book by Asha Lemmie?
Options:
A. I'm starting to feel comfortable nowadays. I can approach a street vendor and know if he's asking for too high a price; I can understand vulgar humor or pick up the meaning of a song when I pass a street performer. On the way to the accommodation, a nice-looking old gentleman with skin so dark it's nearly navy, shiny like the patent leather on good new footwear, lets me beat his conga drum after I gave him some money. The lady beside him smiles a gummy smile at me as she strums her guitar.
B. I am starting to feel comfortable now. I can approach a vendor and know when he is offering me a rate that is too high; I can understand a bawdy joke or pick out the meaning of a song when I pass a musician on the street. On the way to the hotel, a kindly-looking old man with skin so dark that it is nearly blue, shiny like  the patent leather on good new shoes, lets me slap his conga drum after I gave him a tip. The woman beside him smiles a toothless smile at me as she strums her guitar. 
C. I'm beginning to feel at ease these days. I can go up to a merchant and tell if he's quoting me an excessively high rate; I can grasp a bawdy joke or grasp the meaning of a song when I walk past a musician on the street. While heading to the lodging, a kindly-seeming elderly man with skin so dark it's nearly indigo, glossy like the patent leather on nice new shoes, allows me to hit his conga drum after I tipped him. The woman next to him beams a gummy grin at me as she plucks her guitar.
D. I'm beginning to feel at ease now. I can go up to a seller and tell when he's quoting me a price that's too much; I can grasp a crude joke or get the meaning of a song when I walk by a musician on the street. While heading to the hotel, a kindly-appearing elderly man with skin so dark it's nearly blue, glossy like the patent leather on brand new shoes, allows me to hit his conga drum after I tipped him. The woman next to him beams a toothless grin at me as she plucks her guitar.
Answer:
